content.problemStatement:
This is the easy version of the problem. You only need to compute the score for the given array.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN, as well as an integer KKK (2‚Č§K‚Č§N2 \le K \le N2‚Č§K‚Č§N).

A sequence of indices (i1,i2,‚Ä¶,ir)(i_1, i_2, \ldots, i_r)(i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,ir‚Äč) is said to be valid if:


i1<i2<‚Ä¶<iri_1 \lt i_2 \lt \ldots \lt i_ri1‚Äč<i2‚Äč<‚Ä¶<ir‚Äč

That is, the indices are viewed in increasing order.


ij+1‚Č†ij+1i_j + 1 \ne i_{j+1}ij‚Äč+1ÓÄ†=ij+1‚Äč for every 1‚Č§j<r1 \le j \lt r1‚Č§j<r

That is, no two chosen indices are adjacent.


i1‚Č§K,ir‚Č•N‚ąíK+1i_1 \le K, i_r \ge N-K+1i1‚Äč‚Č§K,ir‚Äč‚Č•N‚ąíK+1, and ij+1‚ąíij‚Č§Ki_{j+1} - i_j \le Kij+1‚Äč‚ąíij‚Äč‚Č§K for each 1‚Č§j<r1 \le j \lt r1‚Č§j<r

In simpler words, among any contiguous segment of length KKK, at least one index must be chosen.


Note that in particular, choosing a single index gives a valid sequence (as long as the chosen index is both ‚Č§K\le K‚Č§K and ‚Č•N‚ąíK+1\ge N-K+1‚Č•N‚ąíK+1).

The score of a valid sequence of indices (i1,i2,‚Ä¶,ir)(i_1, i_2, \ldots, i_r)(i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,ir‚Äč) is defined to be

max‚Ā°(Ai1,Ai2,‚Ä¶,Air)‚ąímin‚Ā°(Ai1,Ai2,‚Ä¶,Air)\max(A_{i_1}, A_{i_2}, \ldots, A_{i_r}) - \min(A_{i_1}, A_{i_2}, \ldots, A_{i_r})max(Ai1‚Äč‚Äč,Ai2‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Air‚Äč‚Äč)‚ąímin(Ai1‚Äč‚Äč,Ai2‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Air‚Äč‚Äč)That is, the score equals the maximum among elements at the chosen indices, minus the minimum among elements at the chosen indices.

Given the array AAA and parameter KKK, your task is to compute the **maximum** possible score of a valid sequence of indices.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK ‚ÄĒ the length of the array and the separation parameter.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible score of a valid sequence.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We aren't allowed to choose adjacent elements; and N=2N = 2N=2 so we can only choose any single element.
When choosing a single element, the maximum equals the minimum so their difference is always 000.
So, the answer is 000.
Test case 222: There are five valid sequences, as follows:

(1,3)(1, 3)(1,3), with a score of max‚Ā°(A1,A3)‚ąímin‚Ā°(A1,A3)=8‚ąí4=4\max(A_1, A_3) - \min(A_1, A_3) = 8 - 4 = 4max(A1‚Äč,A3‚Äč)‚ąímin(A1‚Äč,A3‚Äč)=8‚ąí4=4.
(1,4)(1, 4)(1,4), with a score of max‚Ā°(A1,A4)‚ąímin‚Ā°(A1,A4)=8‚ąí3=5\max(A_1, A_4) - \min(A_1, A_4) = 8 - 3 = 5max(A1‚Äč,A4‚Äč)‚ąímin(A1‚Äč,A4‚Äč)=8‚ąí3=5.
(2)(2)(2), with a score of max‚Ā°(A2)‚ąímin‚Ā°(A2)=0\max(A_2) - \min(A_2) = 0max(A2‚Äč)‚ąímin(A2‚Äč)=0.
(2,4)(2, 4)(2,4), with a score of max‚Ā°(A2,A4)‚ąímin‚Ā°(A2,A4)=3‚ąí1=2\max(A_2, A_4) - \min(A_2, A_4) = 3 - 1 = 2max(A2‚Äč,A4‚Äč)‚ąímin(A2‚Äč,A4‚Äč)=3‚ąí1=2.
(3)(3)(3), with a score of max‚Ā°(A3)‚ąímin‚Ā°(A3)=0\max(A_3) - \min(A_3) = 0max(A3‚Äč)‚ąímin(A3‚Äč)=0.

The maximum possible score is hence 555, obtained by the index sequence (1,4)(1, 4)(1,4).
Test case 333: There are only two possible valid sequences: (1,3,5)(1, 3, 5)(1,3,5) and (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4). Their scores are 8‚ąí5=38 - 5 = 38‚ąí5=3 and 12‚ąí9=312 - 9 = 312‚ąí9=3 respectively; so the maximum possible score is 333.
